_vision_explanations:Header & Dates:68c4.1: This is a header section explaining the mail-in method for submitting a moving-out notification (転出届). As a foreign resident, you don't need to write anything here - this is just instructional text explaining that you should fill out this form and mail it to your current ward/city office before moving.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This section is for official use only by municipal staff - leave blank. The section chief or director will stamp and initial here after reviewing your application.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Check the appropriate box that describes your moving situation, such as moving from another city/prefecture within Japan, moving from abroad, or other status changes. Choose only one option that best fits your circumstances.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: This appears to be corrupted text in the image and cannot be accurately translated. If you encounter unclear text on the actual form, ask the municipal office staff for clarification on what information is required here.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: This field is pre-printed and indicates the form is addressed to the Mayor of Katsushika Ward - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: This appears to be part of a pre-printed statement about providing your My Number (individual number) - this is informational text that requires no input from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.2: This field is pre-printed and indicates the form is addressed 'To the Mayor of Katsushika Ward' - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: This appears to be part of a privacy notice about providing personal identification numbers - this is informational text that requires no input from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.4: This field relates to document collection when moving out of your residence. If you're not currently moving out, select '無' (None). If you are moving out and have been given a collection number for document retrieval, select '有' (Yes) and write the number provided by the municipal office.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.5: Select '有' (yes) if you have a transfer collection number from when you moved out of your previous address, or '無' (no) if you don't have one. Most people will select '無' unless they were specifically given a collection number by their previous municipal office.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.9: Select '有' (yes) or '無' (no) regarding whether you need to return a collection number when moving out. This typically relates to returning official documents or ID numbers to the municipal office - most foreign residents should select '無' (no) unless specifically instructed otherwise by city hall.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.9: This field relates to documentation for moving out of your current address. If you are moving out and have been issued a collection number for document retrieval, write that number; otherwise, select '無' (none) if this doesn't apply to your situation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.12: This field relates to moving out documentation and collection numbers for elderly services. If you are not moving out of your current address or don't have any elderly service collection numbers, you would typically circle '無' (none/no).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.2: Circle '無' (none) unless you are moving out and have been given a specific collection number by the municipal office for senior citizen services.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.13: Circle '無' (none) unless you are moving out and have been given a specific collection number by the municipal office for senior citizen services.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 9:.4: This field asks about moving out (転出) - mark 有 (yes) if you are moving out of the current municipality, or 無 (no) if you are not moving out. If marking 有, you may need to provide a collection number (回収No.) related to the moving-out process.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Check this box if you are moving into this ward from another municipality in Japan or from abroad. This is the most common selection for foreign residents establishing residence in a new area.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Check this box if you are moving out of this ward to another municipality or abroad. You would fill this section when leaving your current address permanently.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Check this box for changes within your household such as changing the head of household, combining households, or splitting into separate households. This doesn't involve moving addresses but changes the household structure.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: This indicates the form is addressed to the ward mayor (ku-chō) and is typically pre-printed. No action needed - this shows which official will process your residential registration.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.10: This field is only for people moving into this municipality from another city/town in Japan or from abroad. If you're already a resident of this municipality and just moving within it, leave this section blank.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.1: This section is only for people who are moving into this municipality from another city/town or from abroad. If you're already a resident updating your information, leave this blank.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.12: These are bullet point markers for listing items. You would write information in the spaces after these dots, likely related to your previous address or moving details if you're transferring from another municipality.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.4: Write the birth date in Japanese era format using dots as separators (e.g., 昭和62・3・6 for March 6, 1987). Convert your Western calendar birth date to the appropriate Japanese era year.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.10: Write the birth date in Japanese era format using dots as separators (e.g., 平成元・9・21 for September 21, 1989). This appears to be for a second person, so use the same era conversion method as field 4.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.5: Enter the day portion of a date using Arabic numerals (1-31). This appears to be part of a date field where you fill in the specific day.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.9: Enter the day portion of another date using Arabic numerals (1-31). This is likely for a different date field than field 5, possibly an end date or second reference date.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.10: Check this box or circle if the condition described is 'not yet' applicable or incomplete. This is typically used for pending status or incomplete processes.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.14: Enter the day portion of a date using Arabic numerals (1-31). This appears to be another date field, possibly related to application or processing dates.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.11: Write your phone number in the format shown (4 digits - 4 digits). Use hyphens to separate the numbers, not parentheses like some countries use.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.13: Enter your email address after the @ symbol that's already printed. Make sure to include the full domain name (like gmail.com or yahoo.co.jp).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.1: Write your email address in the format shown, starting with @ symbol followed by your actual email address. Make sure to use a valid email address as this may be used for official communications or verification purposes.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.1: This is a reference note explaining that employment income includes various allowances and benefits beyond base salary. No entry is required here - it's informational text to help you understand what qualifies as employment income.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.7: This shows the basic deduction amount when total income exceeds 25 million yen - which is 0 yen (no deduction). This is likely pre-filled and indicates the deduction disappears completely at very high income levels.

_vision_explanations:Page 6 - Section 1:.1: This section shows the maximum assessment limits for different types of insurance premiums - you don't need to fill anything in here as these are reference amounts set by the government. These limits represent the maximum annual amounts that can be charged for medical insurance (650,000 yen), elderly support insurance (240,000 yen), and long-term care insurance (170,000 yen).

_vision_explanations:Page 7 - Section 1:.3: This is an automatically calculated subtotal for medical benefit contributions (361,920 yen). You don't need to fill this in - it's calculated from the income-based, equal-rated, and flat amounts shown above.
_vision_explanations:Page 7 - Section 1:.4: This is an automatically calculated subtotal for late-stage elderly support contributions (116,807 yen). This amount is derived from the three contribution categories listed in this section.
_vision_explanations:Page 7 - Section 1:.5: This shows the income-based portion of long-term care insurance contributions, calculated as 1,730,000 yen × 0.0256 = 44,288 yen. This is typically auto-calculated based on your reported income.
_vision_explanations:Page 7 - Section 1:.6: This is an automatically calculated subtotal for long-term care insurance contributions (63,072 yen). The system calculates this from the income-based and equal-rated amounts in this section.
_vision_explanations:Page 7 - Section 1:.8: This is the final annual insurance premium total (541,799 yen), calculated by adding the three subtotals: medical benefits + elderly support + long-term care insurance. This represents your total annual health insurance obligation.

_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 2:.3: This is explanatory text ending a sentence about insurance premium reduction determinations, not a field to fill out. No action is needed for this numbered point as it's just informational text explaining that dependent household head income is included when determining premium reductions.

_vision_explanations:Page 9 - Section 1:.3: This is informational text stating who is eligible for the prenatal/postnatal health insurance premium reduction - specifically National Health Insurance subscribers who gave birth or are scheduled to give birth on or after November 1, 2023 (Reiwa 5). No action needed as this is just explaining the eligibility criteria.

_vision_explanations:Page 12 - Section 1.7: This is instructional text, not a field to fill out. It advises that people applying for premium reduction should submit their application before paying the insurance premiums they want reduced, and notes that those using automatic bank transfer need to apply by the end of the month before the premium is debited.

_vision_explanations:Page 13 - Section 1.1: Attach documents that prove unemployment or business suspension (like termination letters, business closure notices) and documents showing your income after these events occurred.
_vision_explanations:Page 13 - Section 1.4: Select this if your income decreased by exactly 100% (meaning you lost all income completely).
_vision_explanations:Page 13 - Section 1.5: Select this if you want exactly 100% reduction of the taxable income amount subject to this exemption.
_vision_explanations:Page 13 - Section 1.6: Select this if your income decreased by 90% or more but not quite 100% (meaning you still have some small income remaining).

_vision_explanations:Page 13 - Section 2.4: This is informational text explaining exclusions from tax reduction eligibility - no action needed. It states that if you've secured company insurance through re-employment or if your income has recovered during the reduction application period, you are not eligible for the tax reduction.

_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.2: This is an informational statement explaining that reduction determinations will be made based on the number of people in your household and their combined income. No action is required from you - this is just explaining how the system calculates potential premium reductions for National Health Insurance.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is the form title - no action needed. It identifies this as Form 2 under Supplementary Provision Article 6 of the National Health Insurance regulations.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This is the form header identifying it as an application for National Health Insurance injury/sickness allowance - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Write the name of the primary policyholder (household head) who holds the National Health Insurance coverage.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write the full name of the injured/sick person who is applying for the allowance benefits.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Write the birth year of the injured/sick person using the Japanese calendar system (e.g., Heisei 5, Reiwa 3) or Western calendar if accepted by your municipality.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Write the birth month and day of the injured/sick person using numbers.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Write today's date when you are submitting this application form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Write the complete current address of the insured person, including postal code, prefecture, city, and apartment details.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Circle either '銀行' (bank), '信金' (credit union), '信組' (credit cooperative), '農協' (agricultural cooperative), or 'その他' (other) to indicate your financial institution type.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Write the name of your bank or financial institution where you want the allowance payments deposited.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write the specific branch name of your bank or financial institution.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Circle '種別' and then specify the account type - usually '普通' (ordinary savings) or '当座' (checking account).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Circle the appropriate account type, with '普通' being the most common for regular savings accounts.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Write your relationship to the insured person if you are not the patient yourself (e.g., spouse, parent, child).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.13: This final calculation line shows your approved benefit amount and is completed by city hall staff after processing your application - do not fill this in yourself.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.4: Write the specific reason for loss or damage of your documents in this parentheses field. For example, write '火災' (fire), '盗難' (theft), '水害' (flood damage), or describe the specific circumstances that caused you to lose your basic pension number notification or maternity allowance exemption certificate.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Write the date that is one day before your actual birthday. For example, if your birthday is March 15th, you would write March 14th here - this follows Japanese legal convention for calculating when age-related changes take effect.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.4: This is a header indicating 'Example/Case Study' - this section shows sample scenarios for when you need to reissue your Basic Pension Number notification. _vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.15: These dots appear to be formatting placeholders in the date field - leave them as they are and don't write over them.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: Write the birth date of the first dependent using the Japanese date format. Use dots to separate year, month, and day (e.g., if born on March 15, 1985, write the Showa year equivalent).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.13: Write the birth date of the second dependent using the same Japanese date format with dots as separators. Convert Western calendar dates to the appropriate Japanese era year (Showa, Heisei, or Reiwa).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: Fill in your birth date using dots to separate year, month, and day (format: year·month·day). Use the Japanese calendar system (Showa, Heisei, Reiwa) as indicated in the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.9: Fill in your birth date using dots to separate year, month, and day (format: year·month·day). This appears to be for a second person or dependent, so use their birth date in the Japanese calendar format.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.5: This appears to be a date field with dots as separators. Fill in the date in Japanese format: year, month, day using numbers.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.12: This is for funeral expenses (葬祭費). Enter the monetary amount for funeral-related costs if applicable to your situation, or leave blank if not relevant.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.4: This appears to be a date field with dot separators. Enter the relevant date in Japanese format (year/month/day) with dots between numbers.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This is the issuing department contact information - Neyagawa City's Citizen Services Department. You don't need to fill this out as it's pre-printed contact information for the office handling overseas medical expense reimbursements.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This is the main telephone number for inquiries about the overseas medical expense system. You can call this number if you have questions about the application process or required documents.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: This is informational text explaining that overseas medical expenses may be partially reimbursed if you receive emergency treatment abroad due to sudden illness or injury.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: This is a warning statement explaining that no reimbursement will be provided if you traveled overseas specifically for medical treatment from the beginning.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.10: You need to obtain Form A (detailed medical certificate) and detailed receipt from the overseas medical institution where you received treatment - these are official documents the hospital/clinic must provide.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.11: Form B refers to the detailed receipt document that must accompany Form A - ensure you get both forms from the medical facility as they are required together for your application.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.1: This is informational text explaining that overseas medical expenses are reimbursed based on standard amounts set by Japanese insurance. No action needed - this is just explaining the reimbursement policy.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.4: This is informational text explaining how reimbursement amounts are calculated (standard amount minus your copay portion). No input required - this section clarifies that you'll receive either the standard amount or actual cost minus copay, whichever is lower.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.7: This is informational text listing medical procedures that are NOT covered by overseas medical expense benefits. No action needed - this warns that organ transplants, fertility treatments, gender reassignment surgery, and chiropractic care are excluded.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.8: This continues the previous sentence about excluded medical treatments, specifically mentioning cutting-edge medical treatments and cosmetic procedures. No input required - just be aware these treatments won't be reimbursed.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: Select '普通' (regular) for standard savings account or '当座' (checking) for checking account - most people use 普通.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.2: Select '当座' only if you have a business checking account, otherwise leave this blank as most people use regular accounts.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.3: Write your bank account number exactly as it appears on your bank book or card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.4: Write your bank's financial institution code (usually 4 digits) - you can find this in your bank book or ask your bank.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.5: Write the account holder's full name exactly as registered with the bank - this must match your bank records perfectly.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.6: Write your bank branch number (usually 3 digits) - found in your bank book or on bank documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.7: Write the account holder's name in katakana characters only, as required for Japanese banking systems.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.8: This section is for maternity allowance application information related to health insurance - complete if applying for childbirth benefits.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.9: The office will stamp this area when they receive your application - leave blank.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.10: Circle '有' (yes) if you were enrolled in social insurance for 6+ months before childbirth, circle '無' (no) if not.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.11: Circle '有' (yes) if you used a midwife for delivery, circle '無' (no) if you delivered at a hospital with doctors.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.12: Circle '有' (yes) if you have additional dependents to include in calculations, circle '無' (no) if not applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.13: Write any additional comments or special circumstances related to your application in this remarks section.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is the form title 'Moving Out Notification by Mail' - no need to fill anything here, it's just the header identifying this as a mail-in form for registering your move out of the city.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Enter the date you are submitting this form in the format: year (年), month (月), day (日) using the Japanese calendar or Western calendar.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Write your current registered address in Atsugi City - this should match exactly what's on your residence card or other official documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write your full name exactly as it appears on your residence card, using the same characters/spelling.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Apply your personal seal (hanko) here, or if you don't have one, you may be able to sign your name - check with city hall first.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Enter your phone number where you can be reached in case there are questions about your moving notification.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Select whether this is for yourself (本人) or if someone else is submitting on your behalf (代理人), and if代理人, provide the required documentation.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Enter the date you actually moved out of your current address, not the date you're filling out the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: You must include copies of identification documents like your residence card, driver's license, My Number card, passport, or health insurance card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Include a pre-paid return envelope with your name written on it so they can send confirmation back to you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: If you have questions, contact Atsugi City Hall's Citizen Affairs Division at the provided phone number 046-225-2110.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.2: Check this box if you are moving out of your current municipality to a different city/town. This is used when you need to officially register your departure from the current address.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: Check this box if you are acknowledging receipt of submitted documents or if this form was sent to you for processing. This is typically used by municipal office staff rather than residents.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This indicates the form was revised on January 1, 2026 - you don't need to write anything here, it's just the form version date.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This section explains where to submit the form - check which office location is most convenient for you (main office or branch).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: This explains procedures specific to Fujisawa City - read this section for local requirements and rules.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: This note clarifies that the Ishikawa branch office is included in the locations where you can submit forms - choose whichever office is more convenient.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: This refers to domestic moving-out notification procedures - select this if you're moving within Japan to a different municipality.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Check this box if you are moving out of Japan (leaving the country permanently or for an extended period). This is specifically for overseas departure registration and should only be marked if you are actually leaving Japan, not just moving to a different address within Japan.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.1: This indicates you are moving out of Japan (overseas departure). Check this box if you are leaving Japan permanently or for an extended period.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: This is informational text - your residence card will be automatically canceled on your departure date, so you should dispose of your registration certificate accordingly.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: This is informational text - you will need to re-register at your new location after moving in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: This is informational text - if moving overseas, you must complete procedures for continued use of your card and electronic certificate by the day before your scheduled departure date.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.7: This refers to procedures available at Fujisawa/Muraoka Citizen Centers. Contact them if you need assistance with the required procedures.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.8: This is the phone number (0466-50-8269) for the citizen center - call if you have questions about the moving out procedures.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.15: This is the phone number (0466-50-3521) for resident registration inquiries - call for questions about your resident status or registration.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: This is an informational notice stating that if a burden classification certificate is issued, you must submit it to your new municipality when moving. No action is required in this field - it's just an instruction to remember for later.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.6: This appears to be a phone number (0466-50-3575) for inquiries, likely for the municipal office or relevant department. You don't need to write anything here - it's provided for your reference if you need to call with questions.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: This is an informational note stating that if a burden classification certificate is issued, you must submit it to your new municipality when moving in. No action needed - just be aware you may need to present this document later.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.4: This is a phone number (0466-50-3575) for inquiries. You don't need to write anything here - call this number if you have questions about this section.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: This indicates the Welfare General Affairs Division (福祉総務課) as the responsible department. No writing required - this shows which office handles these matters.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.9: Another contact phone number (0466-50-8258) for the welfare division. Keep this number for reference if you need assistance with welfare-related procedures.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.13: Contact number (0466-50-3518) for disability support services. Save this number if you need information about disability-related benefits or services.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.14: Instruction stating 'For details, please contact your destination municipality.' This reminds you to ask your new city office for specific requirements when moving.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: This field indicates that if you have a physical disability handbook (身体障がい者手帳) or rehabilitation handbook (療育手帳), you need to bring the actual handbook with you when you move out/transfer your residence registration. No writing is required - this is an instruction to bring the physical document to the municipal office.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: If you have a physical disability certificate or therapy handbook, bring the actual certificate with you when moving out and complete the transfer-out procedures at the municipal office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.4: When moving into a new municipality, complete the procedures to transfer your disability certificate registration, as benefit standards may vary between different local governments.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.6: This is the phone number 0466-50-3528 for inquiries - you would call this number if you have questions about the procedures mentioned above.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.12: This is the phone number 0466-50-3569 for the childcare division - call this number for questions about childcare-related procedures.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.13: This indicates the Childcare Division (保育課) - the department responsible for childcare services and related procedures.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.15: If you were using extended kindergarten care services or non-licensed childcare facilities, complete the certification procedures at your destination municipality after moving out on your departure date.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: This is a phone number field, likely for the childcare division contact (0466-50-8226). You would typically write your own contact phone number here if this is an application form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.2: This section explains that moving out of Fujisawa City will terminate your childcare facility usage and withdraw your application. No action needed - this is informational text about the consequences of moving.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.3: This indicates the responsible department (Childcare Division). This appears to be a pre-printed department name, so no writing is required from applicants.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.4: This section is for residents who want to continue using authorized childcare facilities or maintain their application after moving out. Check this box or provide information if you plan to move but want to continue services.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.7: This appears to be a phone number field, likely for contact information. Enter your Japanese phone number in the format shown (with hyphens between number groups).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.9: This field relates to procedures for moving into a new municipality from another area or from abroad. If you're moving from overseas or another Japanese city, you'll need to complete additional registration procedures at your new municipal office after arrival.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.5: Obtain a school attendance certificate and textbook provision certificate from your current school before transferring. These official documents are required to complete the transfer process.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.7: Contact the school district office or municipal education department in the city you're moving to for specific enrollment procedures and required documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.8: Call this Fujisawa city phone number (0466-50-3558) if you have questions about school transfer procedures or need assistance with the paperwork.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.9: Note that transfer documents must be collected at the school, and if you plan to continue attending Fujisawa schools until the end of the academic year after moving out, additional procedures are required.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: This is a department name (Municipal Tax Division) - you would contact this office for municipal tax-related matters, not fill anything in.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: This is the phone number for the Municipal Tax Division - call this number if you have questions about municipal taxes.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: This is informational text about tax procedures when moving - no action needed, just read to understand that if you move out after January 2nd before receiving tax notices, procedures will be handled between municipalities.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: This is another department name (Tax Collection Division) for tax payment issues - contact information only, not a field to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: This is informational text stating that no procedures are required at your new residence - just informational, no action needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.7: This is the phone number for the Tax Collection Division - call this number for tax payment related questions.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.11: This refers to a 1-month infant health checkup subsidy voucher - contact the health department if you need this service for your baby.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.12: This is the phone number for health-related services including infant checkups - call this number for health services information.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.8: This is an informational note, not a field to fill out. It states 'Please contact your destination municipality for details' - this refers to contacting the city/town hall where you're moving to for specific requirements about moving procedures.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.7: If you are applying for or receiving long-term care certification (要介護/要支援), inform the new municipality that you have an application in progress and mention you're moving from Fujisawa.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.11: If you need a care cost reduction certificate (負担限度額認定証), you must submit a new application at your destination municipality after moving.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.14: This is the phone number 0466-50-3580 for inquiries about child welfare services - call if you have questions about procedures related to children.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.1: This is informational text explaining that motorcycles 126cc and above, and 3-4 wheeled light vehicles require procedures at city hall, not at this location. No writing required - just note this important information.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.2: This indicates the Tax Collection Division (納税課) which handles these vehicle procedures. This is a department name, not a field to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.6: This is the phone number 0466-50-3570 for the Tax Collection Division. You can call this number if you have questions about motorcycle or light vehicle registration procedures.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.7: This explains that for motorcycles 126cc and above when moving out, you need to go to the transport bureau or inspection/registration office in your destination municipality. This is instructional text, not a field to complete.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This is a declaration statement that says 'I hereby appoint the above-mentioned person as my representative and delegate the following authority.' You don't need to write anything here - this is just explanatory text that you acknowledge by completing the form.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: Write the agent's full address including postal code, prefecture, city, ward, and building details. This should be the current residential address of the person you're appointing as your representative.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: Write the agent's full name in the correct Japanese order (family name first, given name second). Use the exact name as it appears on their official identification documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Write the agent's birth date in the format shown (triangles for era year, circles for month and day). For foreign residents, convert your Western calendar birth date to the Japanese era system or use Arabic numerals.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: This is a declaration statement that doesn't require filling in - it states 'I appoint the above person as my agent and delegate the following authority.' Leave this text as-is.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.5: This is a header for the delegation section - no action needed. The specific powers being delegated are listed below this heading.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: This shows examples of what can be delegated - requesting resident certificates and matters related to residence record transfers. These are pre-printed examples, so no writing needed here.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.7: Write the date when you're creating this power of attorney document. Use the same Japanese date format as field 3, and note this should typically be the same as or close to when you sign the document.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: Write your own full current address as the principal (person granting the power). This should match your registered address in Japan exactly as it appears on your residence card.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.9: Write your own full name as it appears on your residence card or other official Japanese documents. Use the same name format as registered with Japanese authorities.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.10: This note explains that the principal must sign themselves, or use a registered seal if they cannot write. Foreign residents should typically sign by hand rather than use a seal unless they have registered one with authorities.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is just a header explaining the method for moving out notification by mail - no need to fill anything in this section.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: This is the title of the form section for 'Moving Out Notification by Mail' - no action required, just the form header.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Write the date you plan to move out of your current address in the format: year, month, day using Japanese calendar or Western calendar as indicated on the form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Write the contact information (phone number, email, etc.) of someone who can reach you during business hours if the office needs to follow up on your application.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Fill in the date you are moving out of your current address in year/month/day format using Japanese calendar or Western calendar as specified on the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Only fill in this address section if someone else (a representative) is submitting this moving notification on your behalf - write the address of the person actually submitting the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write your new address where you will be living after the move.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Write your current address where you are living now before the move.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.11: This is an important note stating that if you mail this notification form, you must provide a contact number where you can be reached during weekday business hours. Make sure to include a phone number where Japanese officials can contact you during standard working hours (typically 9 AM - 5 PM on weekdays).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: This shows the standard per-capita amount of 21,500 yen that applies to each household member. This amount is typically pre-filled and should not be changed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.8: This refers to a reduction amount for preschool children if applicable to your household. Check the reference section mentioned to find the specific reduction amount for any children under school age.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: This will show the final calculated total for the medical portion of your insurance premium with amounts under 100 yen rounded down. This field is automatically calculated from the formula below.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.10: This is the calculation formula that adds income-based amounts (①②③) and subtracts deduction amounts (④⑤) to determine your total medical insurance premium. The system will perform this calculation automatically.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Enter the total medical portion amount with any amount under 100 yen rounded down to the nearest 100 yen.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: Calculate and enter the total by adding amounts from fields ①, ②, and ③, then subtracting amounts from fields ④ and ⑤.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: This will be automatically calculated as 2.60% of the amount in field ⑥ - you typically don't need to fill this manually.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.15: This appears to be a pre-filled standard deduction amount of 9,400 yen - leave as is unless instructed otherwise.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.3: Enter 9,400 yen, which is the standard per capita amount for National Health Insurance tax that applies to most residents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.8: This field should reference the reduction amount for preschool children's per capita tax - check the referenced section of the form to find the specific amount if you have children under school age.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.5: This shows the calculation rate of 2.42% that will be applied to determine your nursing care insurance premium. This is a fixed rate set by the government, so you don't need to enter anything here.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.13: Enter 7,100 yen, which appears to be a standard deduction amount for nursing care insurance calculations. This seems to be a pre-filled standard amount that applies to your situation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.3: This shows the standard amount of 7,100 yen for long-term care insurance. This is a pre-filled amount that you don't need to change.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.6: This will show your total long-term care insurance amount after calculations, with amounts under 100 yen rounded down. This field is calculated automatically from the formula above.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.8: This summarizes the total calculation: Medical portion A + Latter-stage elderly portion B + Long-term care portion C equals your total National Health Insurance tax. This is an automatic calculation field.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: This is a section header explaining the reduction criteria and rates for National Health Insurance premiums - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: This explains that households below certain income thresholds receive equal reductions on medical, elderly support, and long-term care portions - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: This is a table header showing reduction rates and income criteria - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: This shows the 70% reduction rate - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.5: This shows the income threshold for 70% reduction: base amount plus 100,000 yen per pension/salary earner - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: This shows the 50% reduction rate - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.7: This shows the income threshold for 50% reduction: base amount plus additional calculation including insured persons - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: This shows the 20% reduction rate - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.9: This shows the income threshold for 20% reduction: base amount plus higher additional amounts per insured person - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.10: These are footnotes explaining calculation details and special cases - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.11: This is a section header for preschool children's per-capita tax reduction - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.12: This explains that preschool children receive a 50% reduction on their per-capita portion to support child-rearing households - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.13: This explains how the reduction amount varies based on the household's income-based reduction rate - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.14: This is a table header for the preschool children reduction amounts - no entry required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.15: This column shows the reduction amounts after applying preschool children discounts - no entry required.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.12: Mark with ○ if you received expensive medical treatment in November. After the fourth visit, your co-payment amounts will be reduced for subsequent treatments.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is just the form title indicating it's Form No. 7 for long-term care insurance applications - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: This is a formal statement meaning 'I hereby apply as follows' - no action needed, it's just standard legal language on the form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: Select either '1' or '2' to indicate your long-term care need level (要介護状態区分). Circle or mark the number that corresponds to your current certified care level - these range from 1 (lowest need) to 2 (higher need) in this section, with your specific level determined by prior assessment.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.4: This indicates the age range (40-64 years old) for the second category of insured person. If you fall within this age group, you would be classified as a Category 2 insured person, which typically relates to specific healthcare coverage for middle-aged adults in Japan's healthcare system.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: Check this box if you are the second insured person (ages 40-64) applying for specified disease coverage under long-term care insurance.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.2: Write the name of the specific disease or medical condition you are applying for coverage under the long-term care insurance system.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.3: This indicates the age range (40-64 years old) for second category insured persons - no action needed as it's just informational text.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.4: This is a consent clause - by signing below, you agree to allow sharing of your care plan, medical records, and assessment information between relevant agencies and medical professionals.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.5: This appears to be a continuation of the consent text - no separate action required as it's part of the same clause as item 4.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.6: Write the full name of the insured person (the person applying for long-term care insurance coverage).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.7: Write the name of the person filling out this form if different from the insured person (such as a family member or legal representative).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is the form title and legal reference - no action needed, it's pre-printed information identifying this as Form 32 for long-term care insurance contribution limit recognition applications.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Check the box that matches your household registration status: '同一世帯' (same household) if you live with family members who are also registered at the same address, '別世帯(市内)' (separate household within city) if you live alone or separately within the same city, or '別世帯(市外)' (separate household outside city) if your family is registered in a different municipality.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.1: Write the current year, month, and day when you are filling out this form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.2: Write your full name in katakana characters as it appears on your residence card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: Enter your Individual Number (My Number) - the 12-digit number from your My Number card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: Write your current address as of January 1st of the tax year.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: Enter your insured person number from your health insurance card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.6: Write your current address where you actually live now.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.7: Provide a contact phone number where you can be reached.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.8: Check the 課税 (taxable) box if you pay resident tax, or 非課税 (non-taxable) if you don't.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: Check this box if you are a non-taxable resident for municipal taxes and meet the income requirements listed below.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.13: Check this box if you receive public assistance (seikatsu hogo).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.14: This is a header about survivor's and disability pensions - check the relevant boxes below if applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.15: Check this box if you received survivor's pension, widow's pension, or other listed pensions during Reiwa 6 (2024).

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: This is informational text explaining that 'spouse' in this application includes spouses from different households and common-law partners (de facto marriage). No action needed - just read and understand the definition.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.11: Write the date when your illness or injury occurred, using Japanese date format.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.12: Write the specific name of your illness or injury as diagnosed by the doctor - refer to attached medical documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.13: Write the period during which you received medical treatment, typically showing start and end dates.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.14: This appears to be a continuation field - write additional medical treatment period information if the treatment extended beyond the first period listed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.15: Write additional illness or injury names if you were treated for multiple conditions - refer to your medical documents for the exact terminology used.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.3: These dots are for writing dates in Japanese format (year/month/day). Fill in the numbers for when this form was processed or submitted.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.12: Enter the date when the bank account information was verified or confirmed. Use Japanese date format with numbers in each dot position.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.13: Write the date when the direct deposit setup was completed or activated. Fill in year, month, and day in the respective dot positions.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.14: Enter the date related to the salary/benefit payment processing. This likely refers to when your payment information was finalized in the system.

f71a9f6f19c7a218ff26f9e6de2d445f.en:
I notice there's a discrepancy in your request. You mentioned this is text from a Japanese municipal residence registration form (住民異動届), but the text you provided "chromosomal abnormalities" is already in English and would not appear on a Japanese government form.

Could you please provide the actual Japanese text that appears on the form? Japanese municipal residence registration forms typically contain fields like:
- 氏名 (name)
- 住所 (address) 
- 世帯主 (head of household)
- 続柄 (relationship to head of household)
- etc.

Once you provide the Japanese text, I'll be happy to translate it in the exact format you requested.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is just a header/title for the International Classification of Diseases table used by Japan's National Health Insurance system - no input required from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This is a category header for blood and immune system diseases - you don't need to fill anything here, it's just organizational.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This is a category header for infectious and parasitic diseases - no action needed, just a classification label.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: This continues the description of category III diseases - no input required, it's part of the classification system.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: This is the Japanese translation of the blood and immune system diseases category - no filling required.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: This is a subcategory code for sexually transmitted infections - you don't select this unless specifically diagnosed with such conditions.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: This is the Japanese description for sexually transmitted diseases - only relevant if you have a specific diagnosis in this category.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: This is a category header for endocrine, nutritional and metabolic disorders - no input needed unless you have related conditions.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: This refers to viral infections affecting skin and mucous membranes - only select if you have a specific diagnosis like herpes or similar skin viral conditions.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: This is the Japanese translation for endocrine, nutritional and metabolic diseases - only relevant if you have conditions like diabetes, thyroid disorders, or nutritional deficiencies.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: This completes the description of viral skin/membrane infections - select only if specifically diagnosed with such viral conditions by a doctor.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Check this box if you have or have had viral hepatitis (types A, B, C, etc.). This includes both acute and chronic forms, so indicate if you've ever been diagnosed with any viral liver infection.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Check this box if you have any fungal infections, including skin conditions like athlete's foot, nail fungus, or more serious systemic fungal infections. Include both current infections and significant past fungal diseases that required medical treatment.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.2: Check this if you have any fungal infections like athlete's foot, yeast infections, or other mycoses that affect your health condition.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: Select this code if you're dealing with ongoing health problems that resulted from a previous infectious disease or parasitic infection.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: This covers long-term complications from past infections - mark if you have lasting effects from diseases like hepatitis, tuberculosis, or parasitic infections.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: Choose this if you have been diagnosed with dementia caused by blood vessel problems or dementia of unknown cause.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.8: Select this category if you have mental health or behavioral issues caused by alcohol, drugs, or other psychoactive substances.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: This specifically refers to mental disorders from using drugs, alcohol, or other mind-altering substances - include addiction or withdrawal symptoms.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.11: Choose this code if you have been diagnosed with schizophrenia, related psychotic disorders, or persistent delusional disorders.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.12: Select this specific code if you have been diagnosed with stomach cancer or malignant tumors in the stomach.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.13: This is the Japanese translation for stomach cancer - mark if you have any malignant tumors or cancer affecting your stomach.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.14: This is the Japanese term for schizophrenia and related disorders - check if you have these psychiatric conditions.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.15: Mark this if you have been diagnosed with colon cancer or any malignant tumors in your large intestine.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.2: This is a medical code for cancer of the rectosigmoid junction (where sigmoid colon meets rectum). Only select this if you have been diagnosed with this specific type of colorectal cancer by a doctor.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: This is the Japanese translation of the above medical condition. It refers to malignant tumors in the rectosigmoid junction and rectum area.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: This is a medical code for liver cancer and cancer of bile ducts inside the liver. Only select if you have been medically diagnosed with hepatic or intrahepatic bile duct cancer.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: This is the Japanese translation for liver and intrahepatic bile duct cancer. Select only if you have an official medical diagnosis of this condition.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.7: This is a medical code for cancers of the respiratory system including trachea, bronchus and lungs. Only select if diagnosed with cancer in these breathing-related organs.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: This is a pre-printed medical code for trachea, bronchus and lung cancer - you don't need to write anything here as it's just a reference code.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.4: This is the Japanese translation of the above medical condition - again, this is pre-printed reference text that you don't fill in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: This is a pre-printed label for breast cancer in Japanese - no action needed as it's just a reference category.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.8: This is a pre-printed label for uterine cancer in Japanese - this is reference text, not a field to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.9: This is a pre-printed medical code for Alzheimer's disease - you don't write anything here as it's a reference code.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.10: This is the Japanese term for Alzheimer's disease - this is pre-printed reference text, not something you fill in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.11: This is the Japanese term for malignant lymphoma - this appears to be a pre-printed category label that you don't need to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.14: This is a pre-printed medical code for cerebral palsy and paralytic syndromes - you don't need to write anything as it's a reference code.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: Check this box if you have been diagnosed with disorders of the autonomic nervous system, such as dysautonomia, orthostatic hypotension, or other conditions affecting involuntary bodily functions like heart rate, blood pressure, or digestion.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.5: Select this option if you have other neurological conditions not specifically listed in the previous categories, such as rare neurological disorders or conditions that don't fit the standard classifications above.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: Check this if you have vision problems like nearsightedness, farsightedness, or astigmatism that require glasses or contact lenses.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: Mark this for acute upper respiratory infections like common colds, laryngitis, or throat infections (not including flu or pneumonia).
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Select this for outer ear problems like ear infections, earwax blockage, or external ear canal issues.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: Check this if you have bronchitis but it's not specified whether it's acute (short-term) or chronic (long-term).
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.5: Mark this for middle ear problems like ear infections behind the eardrum or issues with the small bones in your ear.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: Select this if you have chronic lung diseases that make breathing difficult, such as COPD or emphysema.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.7: This is a category header for digestive system diseases - don't mark this directly, but use it to find specific digestive conditions below.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: This is a category header for heart and blood vessel diseases - don't mark this directly, but use it to find specific cardiovascular conditions below.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.9: Check this if you have gum disease, gingivitis, or other problems affecting your gums and the tissues around your teeth.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.10: Mark this if you have inflammation of the stomach (gastritis) or small intestine (duodenitis), often causing stomach pain or indigestion.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.11: Select this for serious conditions where blood vessels to the brain are blocked, potentially causing stroke symptoms.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.12: Check this if you have chronic liver inflammation (hepatitis) that doesn't fit into other specific hepatitis categories.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.13: Mark this for other brain blood vessel problems not covered by specific stroke or hemorrhage categories.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.15: Select this if you have consistently low blood pressure that causes symptoms like dizziness or fainting.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.1: Check this box if you have other spinal disorders not listed elsewhere, such as unspecified back problems or spine-related conditions that don't fit other categories.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.2: This refers to abnormal test results (blood tests, urine tests, etc.) that indicate health issues but don't clearly point to a specific disease diagnosis.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.3: Select this for bone density problems like osteoporosis, bone structure abnormalities, or conditions affecting bone strength and formation.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.4: Check this section for general health symptoms and signs you're experiencing that don't fit into specific disease categories above.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.5: Same as field 2 - mark this for abnormal medical test results or laboratory findings that suggest health problems but lack a clear diagnosis.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.7: Select this category for any diseases or disorders affecting your kidneys, bladder, reproductive organs, or urinary system.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 2:.1: This section is for medical conditions identified through lab tests that don't fit into other disease categories. Only mark this if your doctor specifically diagnosed you with a condition based on abnormal lab results that isn't covered elsewhere on the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 2:.3: Check this section if you have kidney, bladder, urinary tract, or reproductive system diseases. Common examples include kidney stones, urinary tract infections, or prostate problems.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 2:.5: Mark this section for injuries from accidents, poisoning, or external causes of harm. This includes fractures, burns, drug poisoning, or any injury caused by something outside your body.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 2:.6: Select this specific code if you have head injuries or internal organ damage from trauma. This is for serious injuries like concussions, brain injuries, or damage to internal organs from accidents.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 2:.7: Choose this for urinary system problems not listed in the specific categories above. This might include rare kidney conditions, unusual bladder disorders, or other urinary tract issues.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 2:.8: Mark this for male reproductive system conditions not specifically listed elsewhere. This covers various prostate, testicular, or other male genital organ conditions beyond common prostate enlargement.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 2:.10: This is just an important notice - code 1503 marked with an asterisk (*) means that particular condition is not covered by Japan's National Health Insurance and you would pay full cost.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This indicates the form type - no action needed as it's pre-printed on the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This is the title section - no action needed as it's pre-printed header text.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write the patient's full name (family name first, then given name), age in years, birth date, and circle either 'Male' or 'Female' for sex.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Enter the specific medical condition or injury name, preferably including the ICD (International Classification of Diseases) code if known.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: This is explanatory text about using ICD codes for National Health Insurance - refer to the separate ICD classification table when filling out field 6.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Write the date when the condition was first diagnosed in Day/Month/Year format using numbers (e.g., 15/03/2024).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Describe the type of medical treatment being provided (e.g., medication, surgery, physical therapy, etc.).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: This is the Japanese translation of 'Type of Treatment' above - the content should match what you write in field 13.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.1: Check either 'Hospitalization' or 'Outpatient or Home Visit' to indicate the type of treatment received. Fill in dates and duration if hospitalized.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.6: Provide a brief description of your medical condition, illness, or injury in simple terms. Keep it concise but clear enough for administrative understanding.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.8: Describe any prescriptions, surgical procedures, or other medical treatments you received in brief. Include major medications or procedures but don't need extensive detail.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.10: Check 'Yes' (はい) if your treatment was due to an accident or injury, or 'No' (いいえ) if it was for illness or routine medical care. This affects insurance coverage.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.12: This refers to a separate detailed itemized bill form (Form B) that lists all medical expenses. You'll need to attach or reference that form here.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.13: Write the total amount of medical expenses as detailed in Form B. This should match the itemized amounts from your hospital bills and physician fees.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.14: Write the full name and complete address of the doctor or hospital that provided your treatment. Include postal code and phone number if space allows.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Write the name of the hospital or clinic where you received treatment in Japanese characters if possible, or romanized letters if necessary.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: Enter the phone number of the medical facility including area code, formatted as Japanese phone numbers typically are (e.g., 03-1234-5678).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: Write the date you visited the medical facility in Japanese format (year/month/day) or the format requested by the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.4: Sign your name as it appears on your official documents - this can be in Roman letters for foreign residents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.5: If the hospital assigned you a patient ID number or medical record number, write it here; leave blank if you don't have one or it's not applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: Write the reference number from your medical records if you have one from the hospital; this is optional as indicated by 'if applicable'.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: Write the full name of the doctor who treated you, preferably in Japanese characters, or ask the hospital staff to help you with the correct spelling.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.8: This is an instruction note telling you to attach Japanese translations and detailed materials for sections 6 and 7 if you have them available.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This is just a header section explaining procedures for Funabashi City - no action needed, just read for information about the process.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This is another header indicating the required documents list below - review the list carefully to ensure you have all necessary items before submitting.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: This note explains that if you have a valid My Number Card or residence card for overseas transfer, you don't need to include a return envelope - simply omit the return envelope if this applies to you.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This is an informational header section titled 'Procedures at Your New Destination' - no action required. It introduces information about what card holders need to do after moving, specifically about continuing to use valid My Number cards or resident registration cards at the new municipal office.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: This is an instruction, not a field to fill out. It tells you that if you're applying for a My Number Card while moving, your application in Funabashi will be canceled and you must reapply at your new municipality after moving.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: This is an informational note, not a field to fill out. It instructs you that when moving to a new municipality, you must apply for a new My Number card at the municipal office in your destination city/ward/town.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: This is a header indicating the form is addressed to the Mayor of Funabashi City - no action needed, it's pre-printed.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: Write your full name in katakana or the same script as used elsewhere in the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Leave this contact information field blank unless specifically requested by the ward office.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: Write a daytime phone number where you can be reached for questions about your move.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.5: This is a pre-printed header about moving date - no action needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: Check this box if you cannot submit your moving notification within 14 days of moving.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.7: Check this box if you are moving again within 6 months of your previous move from the old address.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: Check this box for other special circumstances and write the reason below.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.9: Write the specific reason for late submission, such as '仕事の都合で' (due to work circumstances).
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.10: Write the reason for your relocation, such as job transfer or family circumstances.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.11: This is a pre-printed header about your new address - no action needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.12: Write your complete new postal code in the format XXX-XXXX.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.13: Write your complete new address including city, ward, district, and building details.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.14: Write the name of the head of household at your new address (usually yourself or spouse).

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: Write the name of your city or municipality where you currently live. For foreign residents, this should match the city listed on your residence card (zairyu card) - common examples include Tokyo-to, Osaka-shi, Yokohama-shi, etc.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.2: Write your full name in katakana characters. If you're a foreign resident, you should use the katakana version of your name as it appears on your residence card or other official Japanese documents.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.1: This is the title section for 'Moving Out Notification by Mail' - no writing required here, just the form header indicating this is for people moving out of the city who cannot submit the notification in person.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.15: This refers to writing information outside the designated boxes when there's insufficient space - use the margins or blank areas to continue any information that doesn't fit in the provided fields, such as long addresses or additional family members.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This is informational text explaining that if you use the online moving-out procedure, you don't need to visit the office or mail documents. No writing required - just read and understand this benefit.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Fill in your address and affix the necessary postage stamp to the return envelope for official correspondence.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Send this to your old address or new address as specified in the instructions - this ensures you receive important government communications.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: If you are moving overseas/leaving Japan permanently, you do not need to submit this return envelope.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: If you have a valid My Number card, you can likely skip this envelope requirement as digital communication may be available.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.1: Bring your National Health Insurance card or qualification notification document if you are enrolled in National Health Insurance.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.2: Bring your qualification confirmation document if you are enrolled in Late-Stage Elderly Medical Insurance (for those 75 and older).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: Bring your Long-Term Care Insurance certificate if you are enrolled in Long-Term Care Insurance.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: Bring your medical expense subsidy voucher if you are eligible for children's medical assistance.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: A power of attorney is required when someone else is handling the procedure on your behalf.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.6: The power of attorney must be filled out by everyone who is moving out of Narita City, regardless of age.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.7: You must attach identity verification documents for the representative (person acting on behalf of the applicant).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.8: A power of attorney is not needed if the representative is a member of the same household registered in Narita City.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: This is contact information - you don't need to write anything here, this is just reference information.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.11: This is the address of Narita City Hall in Chiba Prefecture - reference information for where to submit the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.12: This shows the destination department (Narita City Hall, Citizens Affairs Division) - reference information only.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: This is an instruction, not a field to fill out. It says if your residence card is lost/damaged or expired with more than 14 days having passed since moving, mark 'No' for having a residence card and attach a return envelope with postage.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.9: Enter the birth date for the first person moving out by writing the year, month, and day in the provided spaces after selecting the appropriate era (大正/昭和/平成/令和).
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.11: Enter the birth date for the second person moving out using the same format as above - select the era and fill in year, month, and day.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.13: Enter the birth date for the third person moving out following the same pattern - choose the appropriate Japanese era and write the corresponding year, month, and day.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.2: Enter the era year and month (e.g., for Reiwa 5, write 5 in the first dot and the month number in the second dot). Use the Japanese era calendar system, not Western calendar.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.4: Write the era year and month when you completed your moving-out procedures from your previous address. This should match the date on your previous residence certificate.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.6: Enter the era year and month when you completed your moving-out procedures. This appears to be a duplicate field for confirmation - use the same date as field 4.
