_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is explanatory text stating that National Health Insurance is one type of public medical insurance in Japan - no action needed from you as it's just informational.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This section lists the benefits you can receive through National Health Insurance - it's informational content showing what medical costs and services are covered, so no input is required from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: This is contact information for Narashino City's National Health Insurance department, not a field to fill out. Use this phone number (047-451-1151) if you need help with your application or have questions about national health insurance.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.10: This is informational text stating that people aged 70-74 have a validity period until July 31, Reiwa 8 (2026). No action needed - this is just explaining the expiration date for your qualification notice.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.14: This is a clarification note explaining that people turning 75 years old can use their qualification notice until the day before their 75th birthday. No writing required - this is just additional information about the validity period.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.3: This is informational text explaining that the self-payment ratio listed in your qualification certificate will be applied to your medical expenses. No action needed - this just confirms which percentage you'll pay.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.6: This indicates a 30% self-payment ratio applies to you based on your income level. This is informational only - no writing required, but note you'll pay 30% of medical costs.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.8: This explains that when multiple National Health Insurance members live together, the self-payment ratio is determined by the person with the highest taxable income. No input required - this is explanatory text about calculation methods.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.10: This clarifies that income calculations use pre-deduction amounts (not net income) and include various income sources like real estate and stocks. This is reference information - no writing needed, but helpful for understanding how your ratio was determined.

_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.4: This indicates the month following your medical treatment when the high-cost medical expense calculation begins. You don't need to write anything here - it's just explaining the timeline.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.9: This explains that even when visiting multiple departments at the same hospital, costs are calculated separately by department. No action needed - this is informational text about the calculation rules.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.11: This refers to private room fees that insurance doesn't cover. You don't write anything here - it's explaining what costs are excluded from the high-cost medical benefit calculation.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.12: This is the end of a sentence explaining exclusions from the calculation. No input required - it's part of the instructional text.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.13: This refers to how medical costs are calculated for the benefit. No writing needed - it's explaining the calculation process.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.14: This shows 'January' as an example of how to calculate monthly periods (1st to 31st). You don't need to write anything - it's illustrating how months are counted for the benefit calculation.

_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.4: This shows the maximum out-of-pocket medical expense limit for high-income households (over 9.01 million yen annually). You don't fill this in - it's reference information showing you'll pay 252,600 yen plus 1% of costs above 842,000 yen.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.6: This indicates the cost-sharing limit for middle-income households (6.007-9.01 million yen annually). This is also reference information - you pay 167,400 yen plus 1% of medical costs exceeding 558,000 yen.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.13: This shows the same high-income cost limit as field 4, but applies to people aged 70+ whose municipal tax standard amount is 6.9 million yen or above. Again, this is informational - no input required from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.1: This shows the maximum out-of-pocket medical expense limit for high-income earners (municipal tax assessment of 3.8-6.9 million yen). You don't need to write anything here - this is informational text explaining that you pay 167,400 yen plus 1% of any amount exceeding 558,000 yen in total medical costs.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.2: This continues the explanation from field 1, indicating the 1% additional charge applies when total medical expenses exceed 558,000 yen. This is also informational text that doesn't require any input from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.3: This shows the out-of-pocket limit for middle-income earners (municipal tax assessment of 1.45-3.8 million yen). Like the others, this is informational text explaining the payment structure - no input required.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.4: This shows the base amount (80,100 yen plus 1%) for the middle-income bracket. This is reference information only and doesn't require you to write anything.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.5: This explains the 1% additional charge applies when medical expenses exceed 267,000 yen for the middle-income bracket. This is informational text that serves as a reference for understanding your potential costs.

_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 2:.3: This shows the standard monthly premium of 18,000 yen for general income earners. This amount is automatically determined based on your income category, so no action is needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 2:.6: This shows the calculated annual premium of 44,400 yen based on the monthly amount. This is for reference only and will be automatically calculated from your monthly premiums.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 2:.7: This indicates the annual premium cap of 144,000 yen - if your calculated premiums exceed this amount, you'll only pay up to this maximum. This is an automatic system limit that protects you from excessive charges.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 2:.11: This note explains that income calculations use 800,000 yen as the basic deduction until July 31, 2025, then 806,700 yen from August 1, 2025. This affects how your income category is determined but requires no action from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 6 - Section 1:.2: This section explains the medical treatment expense reimbursement system. You don't need to fill anything out here - it's informational text describing when you can receive reimbursement for medical expenses you paid upfront, the required documents (like receipts, doctor's certificates, and bank account information), and that reimbursement is processed within 2 years of receiving treatment.

_vision_explanations:Page 7 - Section 1:.1: This section explains meal cost subsidies during hospitalization - no action needed from you as it's informational. If you qualify for reduced meal costs (categories 2 or 3), apply for the 'Standard Self-Payment Amount Reduction Certificate' at your city hall before hospitalization.
_vision_explanations:Page 7 - Section 1:.12: This explains that childbirth benefits are 500,000 yen (520,000 yen if delivered at a medical facility) - this is informational text only. If you recently quit your job within 6 months of giving birth, you may still be eligible for benefits from your former employer's insurance.

_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.2: This is explanatory text about support funds - no action needed, just read that these funds support the elderly healthcare system.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.3: This is explanatory text about nursing care contributions - no action needed, just read that these apply to people aged 40-64 for long-term care insurance.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.4: This is explanatory text explaining how premium calculations work - no action needed, just read that it explains the total calculation method.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.10: This shows the medical portion income-based rate of 8.1% - this is a reference rate used in calculations, no input required.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.11: This shows the support fund portion income-based rate of 2.6% - this is a reference rate used in calculations, no input required.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.12: This shows the nursing care portion income-based rate of 2.6% - this is a reference rate used in calculations, no input required.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.13: This shows the nursing care portion per-person rate of 15,600 yen - this is a reference amount used in calculations, no input required.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.14: This is explanatory text noting that preschool children pay a reduced rate of 12,200 yen - no action needed, just reference information.

_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 2:.2: This is an explanatory note about deductions, not a field to fill out. It explains that the basic deduction amount (基礎控除額) varies depending on your total income amount and that casualty loss carryforward deductions are not included in this calculation.

_vision_explanations:Page 9 - Section 1:.2: These are the months from July to March of the following year, representing the payment periods for national health insurance premiums. Check the circles corresponding to the months when you need to make payments based on your enrollment timing.
_vision_explanations:Page 9 - Section 1:.5: This is an example showing that someone born on September 10th would mark the circle, indicating when premium payments begin. Use this as a reference for understanding how birth dates affect payment schedules.
_vision_explanations:Page 9 - Section 1:.6: This indicates 'Please pay up to the 5th period.' Follow the arrow and instructions to understand how many payment periods apply to your specific situation based on your enrollment date.
_vision_explanations:Page 9 - Section 1:.12: This shows July as a reference month in the payment schedule example. Use this to understand the timing of when different premium components become due.
_vision_explanations:Page 9 - Section 1:.13: This shows January as part of the payment timeline extending into the following year. Note that the insurance year runs from April to March, so January payments are part of the current insurance year cycle.

_vision_explanations:Page 10 - Section 1.4: This shows July 31st as the payment due date for Period 1 insurance premiums. No action needed - this is just reference information about when your first period payment is due.
_vision_explanations:Page 10 - Section 1.8: This shows February 2nd as the payment due date for Period 7 insurance premiums. No action needed - this is reference information showing when your seventh period payment is due.

_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.6: Enter '3回' (3 times) to indicate this is the 3rd payment of special tax collection for July. This corresponds to the 3rd installment in the special collection schedule.
_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.7: Enter '5回' (5 times) to indicate this is the 5th payment of special tax collection for December. Follow the sequential numbering shown in the payment schedule above.
_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.8: Enter '6回' (6 times) to indicate this is the 6th and final payment of special tax collection for February. This completes the 6-installment special collection cycle.
_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.9: Enter '1回' (1 time) to indicate this is the 1st payment when transitioning from regular collection back to special collection. This marks the beginning of a new collection cycle.
_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.12: Write 'となります。' to complete the sentence explaining that February's tax amount becomes the provisional collection amount for the following fiscal year.
_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.14: Enter '1期2期3期' (Period 1, Period 2, Period 3) to indicate the three periods of regular tax collection from July to September before switching to special collection in October.
_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.15: Enter '1回' (1 time) to show this is the 1st payment when transitioning from regular collection (October start) to special collection system in April of the following year.

_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 2.2: This is informational text stating that regular collection (普通徴収) will apply - no action needed from you as this is just explaining the collection method that will be used for your taxes.

_vision_explanations:Page 12 - Section 1.5: This is an informational note explaining that the calculation method applies when there are 2 or more wage earners in the household with income exceeding specified thresholds (600,000 yen for under 65, 550,000 yen for 65+). If there's only one such earner or fewer, use only the upper calculation method - no action needed from you as this is just an instruction.

_vision_explanations:Page 13 - Section 1.4: This is just an informational note explaining that the colored sections in the chart above show the periods when insurance premiums are exempted - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 13 - Section 1.7: This is informational text explaining that if you need emergency medical care and cannot afford the full cost, you may be eligible for reduced medical fees (30% instead of 100%) based on your circumstances - contact your local office if this applies to you.

_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.1: This is just a header explaining that specific health checkups are the first step to good health - no action needed from you here.
_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.2: This shows that health checkups worth 10,000 yen are available for free - this is informational only, you don't need to write anything.
_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.3: This lists the basic examination items included (medical interview, physical measurements, physical exam, blood pressure) - no input required from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.6: This refers to optional electrocardiogram testing that may be recommended based on your basic checkup results - no action needed here.
_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.7: This refers to optional eye fundus examination that may be recommended based on your basic checkup results - no action needed here.
_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.8: This explains you can receive group health checkups at the Health Center 4 times per year with advance registration - note this option if you prefer group settings.
_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.12: This explains that people requiring 'proactive support' or 'motivational support' will be assigned to health professionals - no action needed, this is automatic based on your results.
_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.13: This is part of the explanation about health support services - no input required from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.14: This encourages using health guidance as an opportunity to review your diet and lifestyle habits - no action needed, this is advisory text.

_vision_explanations:Page 14 - Section 1.1: Choose either Method A (submit a copy of your health checkup results and questionnaire filled out in the window to the Health Support Division) or Method B (apply through the Chiba Electronic Application Service and submit via email). For foreign residents, Method A may be simpler as it involves direct submission to the office, while Method B requires online application but may be more convenient if you're comfortable with digital submission.

_vision_explanations:Page 15 - Section 1.1: This field is for office use only - it shows when your application was received by the city office. You don't need to fill this out yourself.
_vision_explanations:Page 15 - Section 1.5: This is informational text explaining that if you apply in person at the city office window, they will issue your voucher immediately on the spot.
_vision_explanations:Page 15 - Section 1.8: This shows the subsidy limits for ages 65-74: up to 26,000 yen for internal medicine examinations and up to 4,000 yen for eye examinations when receiving care at different medical departments.

_vision_explanations:Page 16 - Section 1.6: These are the payment periods (July through March) shown in the table header. No action needed - this is just labeling the months for reference.
_vision_explanations:Page 16 - Section 1.7: This explains the premium calculation example: a person turning 75 in September pays 5 months of regular health insurance (April-August) plus their spouse pays 12 months. This is informational only.
_vision_explanations:Page 16 - Section 1.8: This shows that the same person turning 75 pays 7 months of elderly medical insurance (September-March) after transitioning to the new system. No input required - this is an explanation.
_vision_explanations:Page 16 - Section 1.9: This clarifies that while payment periods may overlap during the transition, you won't be double-charged. This is just an important note to understand, not a field to fill out.

_vision_explanations:Page 18 - Section 1.1: This is an informational warning, not a field to fill out. It advises that you should contact the National Pension Office before accepting any medical payment settlements, as the settlement terms might unexpectedly create financial burdens for you as the victim.

_vision_explanations:Page 19 - Section 2.1: This appears to be part of a health recommendation about developing habits of regular physical movement. If this is a fillable field, you would write about your exercise or physical activity habits.
_vision_explanations:Page 19 - Section 2.2: This is an encouraging phrase meaning 'let's do it' - likely the end of a health recommendation sentence. If fillable, it may be asking for your commitment or agreement to follow the advice.
_vision_explanations:Page 19 - Section 2.3: This is a strong anti-smoking message stating tobacco has only harms and no benefits. This appears to be informational text rather than a field to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 19 - Section 2.4: This encourages getting regular health checkups to maintain a healthy lifestyle. If this is a fillable section, write about your plans or commitment to regular medical checkups.
_vision_explanations:Page 19 - Section 2.5: This discusses creating a support network of non-smoking friends/companions. If fillable, you might write about building relationships with others who support healthy, smoke-free living.

_vision_explanations:Page 20 - Section 1.1: This is not a field to fill out - it's part of the document title/header explaining that living healthy and vibrant days is everyone's wish.
_vision_explanations:Page 20 - Section 1.2: This is also part of the introductory text, not a fillable field - it completes the thought that healthy living is our collective aspiration.
_vision_explanations:Page 20 - Section 1.4: This label indicates medical expenses that you pay out of pocket (your personal portion after insurance coverage).
_vision_explanations:Page 20 - Section 1.6: This refers to the portion of medical expenses paid by the insurance system/insurer, not something you need to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 20 - Section 1.7: This means 'to pay' and is part of the process description - not a field requiring input from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 20 - Section 1.3: This is informational text explaining that in National Health Insurance, each person in the household (both adults and children) becomes an insured person individually. No action needed - just read and understand this policy.
_vision_explanations:Page 20 - Section 1.5: This is just a page number indicator showing page 20. No input required from you - this is pre-printed on the form.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: This is an informational field about liver function tests that will be performed - no action needed from you as it's just describing what blood tests will be done.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: This describes the anemia screening tests (blood cell counts) that are included - again, this is informational only and requires no input from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.5: This indicates that an electrocardiogram (heart rhythm test) is included in the health checkup - no action required, just informational.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: This section tells you to call the medical institution directly to make an appointment - choose a facility from pages 8-11 and contact them by phone.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: You need to bring the green-colored medical examination voucher and questionnaire that should have been mailed to you - complete the questionnaire beforehand.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.9: Bring the light blue medical examination ticket along with your health insurance card or residence card to verify your eligibility for national health insurance.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.11: Results will be available after approximately one week from the medical institution where you took the exam - schedule a follow-up appointment to discuss results with the doctor.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.12: This is the contact number (048-400-2993) for the Specific Health Checkup Call Center if you have questions about the program.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.13: This indicates where to direct general inquiries - use the call center number above for any questions about the health checkup process.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.1: This is contact information for the Specific Health Checkup Call Center - no action needed from you, this is provided for reference if you need help.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.2: This section header indicates where to direct inquiries or questions - the phone number 048-400-2993 is provided for contacting the health checkup call center if you need assistance.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 2:.1: This appears to be asking you to select 'Yes' or 'No' for a question about something (はい). Check the appropriate box - the left box for 'Yes' (はい) or leave it blank/check the right box if the answer is 'No'. Make sure to use diagonal lines when marking boxes as indicated in the instructions.

_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.3: This refers to physical measurements including height, weight, BMI, and waist circumference that will be taken during your health checkup - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.4: This is an anemia screening test that checks red blood cell count, hemoglobin levels, and hematocrit - it's included automatically in the blood work.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.5: An electrocardiogram (ECG/EKG) test to check your heart rhythm and electrical activity - this is a standard test included in the comprehensive checkup.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.7: This is a stool test to detect hidden blood that may indicate colon issues - you'll receive a collection kit and instructions from the medical facility.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.10: A blood test for syphilis detection - this is automatically included in the expanded health screening package for eligible residents.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.11: Choose a medical facility from the list on pages 8-11 and call them directly to make an appointment - confirm any special instructions when you call.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.12: Refer to pages 8-11 for the complete list of participating hospitals and clinics where you can receive this health screening.

_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 2:.2: This refers to the green medical examination voucher and questionnaire that you need to bring with you. You don't need to write anything here - just make sure to bring the actual green voucher form to your medical appointment.
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 2:.5: This section explains how you'll receive your test results. You don't need to fill anything out here - it's just informational about the results delivery process from the medical facility.

_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.1: This is an informational header - no action needed. It indicates the section is for people identified as having metabolic syndrome risk.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.2: This is informational content explaining metabolic syndrome - no writing required. Read to understand the condition involves accumulated visceral fat affecting lifestyle habits.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.3: This describes support types available - no writing needed. 'Motivational support' helps you set and achieve realistic health improvement goals.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.7: This is an informational section header about cancer screening systems - no input required from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.8: This shows the Health Promotion Division contact information (048-256-1135) - call if you have questions about health programs.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.9: This indicates the Health Screening Section - the department responsible for health checkups and screenings.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.10: This refers to a QR code link for exemption information - scan if you need fee reductions for cancer screenings based on income.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.11: This refers to a QR code for reissuing screening vouchers - scan if you lost your examination tickets and need replacements.

_vision_explanations:Page 6 - Section 1:.1: This section is specifically for people who are currently receiving treatment for lifestyle-related diseases (diabetes, hypertension, etc.) or have received such treatment in the past. If this applies to you, continue reading this section; if not, you can skip it.
_vision_explanations:Page 6 - Section 1:.2: This explains a system where your regular blood test results from treating lifestyle diseases can be shared with the city to waive health checkup requirements. The system started accepting applications from November 2025 (Reiwa 7) and allows you to receive health benefits without taking separate city health exams.

_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.2: This is the header for cancer screening section. You don't need to fill anything here - it's just indicating the category of medical examinations available.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.4: This refers to cervical cancer screening for women. If you're eligible (typically women 20+ years old), you can receive this screening once every 2 years through the national health insurance program.
_vision_explanations:Page 8 - Section 1:.5: This appears to be incorrectly labeled - it actually refers to gastric (stomach) cancer screening using endoscopy (internal camera examination). This is typically available for adults 50+ years old as part of preventive health screenings.

_vision_explanations:Page 9 - Section 1:.1: This column indicates what type of comprehensive health screening (人間ドック) services are available at this medical facility. You would check or mark this if you want to receive a full-body medical checkup at this clinic.
_vision_explanations:Page 9 - Section 1:.3: This refers to the medical institution that will conduct your health screening. The clinic name 'かがやきクリニック川口' (Kagayaki Clinic Kawaguchi) is already filled in as the implementing agency for health services in this area.

_vision_explanations:Page 10 - Section 1.3: This section is for cancer screening programs. Check the boxes for the types of cancer screenings you want to receive (stomach, cervical, breast, etc.).
_vision_explanations:Page 10 - Section 1.4: This indicates the implementation period is from June to February. You don't need to write anything here - it's just informational about when these health checkups are available.
_vision_explanations:Page 10 - Section 1.5: This shows services are conducted until February. Like item 4, this is informational only - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 10 - Section 1.6: Write the name of the medical institution or healthcare facility where you want to receive your health checkup services.
_vision_explanations:Page 10 - Section 1.7: This is for comprehensive medical checkups (ningen dock). Check this if you want a full-body health screening that's more extensive than basic checkups.

_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.1: This is an informational header - no writing required. It emphasizes the importance of annual health checkups.
_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.3: Check this box if you want to receive a comprehensive medical checkup (ningen dock), which is more thorough than basic health screenings.
_vision_explanations:Page 11 - Section 1.4: Check this box if you want to receive cancer screening tests as part of your health checkup.

_vision_explanations:Page 12 - Section 1.1: This is a header/reference section showing the cancer screening schedule - no writing required, just use it to understand what screenings are available and when.
_vision_explanations:Page 12 - Section 1.3: This shows contact phone numbers for different health services - call these numbers if you have questions about health checkups or cancer screenings (no writing needed).
_vision_explanations:Page 12 - Section 1.10: This indicates stomach X-ray screening using barium contrast - if you're 40 or older, you can choose this option during the specified period (August to February of next year).

_vision_explanations:Page 12 - Section 2.2: This is cervical cancer screening for women aged 20 and over (born before April 1, 2004). Choose between group screening (集団) in September-February or individual screening (個別) available June-February.
_vision_explanations:Page 12 - Section 2.5: This is prostate cancer screening for men only. Select your age group from the specific birth date ranges listed (50, 55, 60, 65, 70, or 75 years old) and choose individual screening (個別) available June-February.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: This is a table of contents section - no input required. It lists the chapters and sections of the health implementation plan document.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: This column shows page numbers where each section begins - no input needed. The circles (○) indicate which sections are relevant to specific health examination programs.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: This chapter header describes health data analysis and issue identification - no input required. It's part of the document structure showing how health problems are identified through data analysis.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: This refers to methods for implementing specific health guidance programs. You would describe concrete approaches like individual counseling, group sessions, or digital health tools used to provide health guidance.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.7: This section covers outreach to people who ignore abnormal test results or stop medical treatment. Describe specific strategies to encourage these individuals to seek proper medical care and continue treatment.

_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.1: Check this box or circle this option if you are moving out of the current municipality to a different city, town, or village in Japan. This is the most common type of moving notification for people relocating to a new area.

_vision_explanations:Page 58 - Section 1.1: Check boxes or write details about current medications, past medical conditions, symptoms you're experiencing, and smoking habits. If you don't smoke or have no medical history, indicate 'none' or leave blank as appropriate.
_vision_explanations:Page 58 - Section 1.2: Enter your height in centimeters, weight in kilograms, BMI (weight÷height²), and waist circumference in centimeters. These are basic physical measurements typically taken during the health check.

_vision_explanations:Page 58 - Section 1.9: This blood test value will be recorded by medical staff and indicates the percentage of red blood cells in your blood.
_vision_explanations:Page 58 - Section 1.10: Medical staff will record results of urine test checking for hidden blood, which can indicate kidney or urinary tract issues.
_vision_explanations:Page 58 - Section 1.11: This blood test result will be filled by staff and measures kidney function - higher levels may indicate kidney problems.
_vision_explanations:Page 58 - Section 1.12: Medical staff will record your blood uric acid level from blood tests - elevated levels can indicate gout risk or kidney issues.

_vision_explanations:Page 59 - Section 1.1: This explains that serum creatinine testing checks kidney function. The city conducts this test for everyone to detect chronic kidney disease (CKD) early, even if you don't meet the detailed health examination criteria.
_vision_explanations:Page 59 - Section 1.4: This describes that ECG testing is part of detailed health examinations. As a foreign resident, you don't need to write anything here - this is informational text explaining when ECG tests are performed.
_vision_explanations:Page 59 - Section 1.5: This explains the criteria for ECG testing: people with high blood pressure results or suspected arrhythmia. You don't need to fill anything out - this is just explaining when you might receive an ECG test.

_vision_explanations:Page 60 - Section 1.1: This is an informational statement, not a field to fill out. It explains that awareness about the importance of health checkups will be promoted through various flyers and posters - no action required from you as a form filler.

_vision_explanations:Page 61 - Section 1.1: This section describes health guidance programs involving individual counseling and group work sessions. No action needed - this is informational text explaining the support services provided.
_vision_explanations:Page 61 - Section 1.2: This defines dyslipidemia criteria for health guidance eligibility. Check if your triglyceride levels meet these thresholds (150mg/dL fasting or 175mg/dL non-fasting) based on your health checkup results.
_vision_explanations:Page 61 - Section 1.3: This sets blood pressure criteria for health guidance programs. You qualify if your systolic pressure is 130mmHg+ or diastolic pressure is 85mmHg+ from your health screening.
_vision_explanations:Page 61 - Section 1.4: Important exclusion note: You don't qualify for this health guidance if you're already taking prescribed medications for diabetes, high blood pressure, or cholesterol problems.

_vision_explanations:Page 62 - Section 1.1: This is an informational statement, not a field to fill out. It explains that health guidance services will be outsourced to specialized providers with diverse guidance methods.
_vision_explanations:Page 62 - Section 1.2: This is also informational, not requiring input. It states that there is no self-payment burden for specific health guidance services - they are provided free of charge.

_vision_explanations:Page 63 - Section 1.1: This field involves extracting health checkup and health guidance data for the current fiscal year. You would typically enter details about which health data records need to be pulled from your medical examinations and any ongoing health guidance programs you're participating in.
_vision_explanations:Page 63 - Section 1.2: Here you select the health guidance institution and analyze performance metrics and implementation methods. Choose from approved healthcare providers in your area and indicate your preferred method of receiving health guidance (in-person, online, etc.).
_vision_explanations:Page 63 - Section 1.3: This section is for reviewing and potentially changing your designated healthcare institutions. If you want to switch to a different clinic or health guidance provider, indicate your new preference and reasons for the change.

_vision_explanations:Page 64 - Section 1.1: Check this if your municipality offers subsidies for comprehensive health checkups (human dock examinations). This is common in Japan where local governments help cover costs for preventive health screenings.
_vision_explanations:Page 64 - Section 1.2: Select this if there are programs to encourage people who don't use the mandatory health guidance services after age 40. This targets those who skip follow-up consultations after health screenings.
_vision_explanations:Page 64 - Section 1.3: Choose this if there are programs to encourage people with abnormal health results to seek treatment or continue their medical care. This addresses those who ignore health warnings or stop treatment.
_vision_explanations:Page 64 - Section 1.5: Check this if your area promotes health management through smartphone apps or digital health tools. Many Japanese municipalities now offer apps to track health metrics and encourage healthy lifestyles.
_vision_explanations:Page 64 - Section 1.6: Select this if there are integrated programs combining health services with nursing care prevention for elderly residents. This reflects Japan's approach to keeping seniors healthy and independent longer.

_vision_explanations:Page 65 - Section 1.3: This field describes collecting medical test data from healthcare institutions as part of treatment records. This is typically a pre-filled description by the government form - you don't need to write anything here as it's explaining what medical institutions will provide to support the health screening program.

_vision_explanations:Page 66 - Section 1.6: These show target percentages for health checkup rates among people in their 40s, starting from the current 22.8% and aiming for 35% by Reiwa 11. These are also predetermined municipal planning targets that don't require input from individual residents.

If you have risk factors like high blood pressure or elevated blood sugar, you may be recommended to participate in these prevention programs.
_vision_explanations:Page 78 - Section 1.4: This indicates which government department handles this program - the Health Promotion Division of your local city/municipal office. Contact this department if you need information about health programs or want to register for classes.
_vision_explanations:Page 78 - Section 1.6: Same as field 4 - this shows that the Health Promotion Division manages multiple health programs including cancer screenings, dental health, and exercise classes. They can provide information in multiple languages for foreign residents.
_vision_explanations:Page 78 - Section 1.8: This is a medical abbreviation explanation noting that CKD stands for chronic kidney disease. If you have kidney problems or risk factors, you may be enrolled in special monitoring and education programs to prevent the condition from worsening.

_vision_explanations:Page 79 - Section 1.1: This appears to be part of a continuous sentence about implementing mid-term evaluations in the third year of the plan. You would typically continue the existing text flow describing the evaluation timeline and process.
_vision_explanations:Page 79 - Section 1.2: This section explains the revision process when targets aren't met. You should describe how your organization will analyze reasons for underperformance and what criteria will be used to determine if program modifications are needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 79 - Section 1.3: Describe how each individual health program in your plan will follow the PDCA (Plan-Do-Check-Act) cycle methodology. Include specific examples of how you'll implement continuous improvement processes for effective health interventions.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.9: Write the amount after monthly division here. This will be calculated based on your annual tax amount divided by the payment periods.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.2: This describes the full-term payment option - if you want to pay your entire annual insurance premium at once, look for a slip marked with '全' (zen/entirety).
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.3: Look for the character '全' on the bottom right of your payment slip to confirm it's for full annual payment.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.4: This describes installment payments - if you want to pay in installments, use slips numbered 1-10 corresponding to each payment period.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.5: This explains that installment payments divide your annual insurance tax into multiple smaller payments throughout the year.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.6: Submit your payment slip at the financial department counter at city hall, along with cash or through their payment system.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.8: Complete the procedure to stop your National Health Insurance participation after leaving Japan or switching to employer insurance - don't forget this step to avoid continued charges.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.13: This refers to stopping insurance for all family members included in your plan - make sure to process termination for everyone covered under your policy.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.2: Write the name of the specific ward citizens' affairs office (区民事務所) or national health insurance division (国保課) where you are submitting this form. You can find this information by checking which office serves your residential area or asking at the counter where you're submitting the document.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.4: This is a notification/notice section that provides instructions or important information about the form. _vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Write your phone number in the parentheses, using Japanese format (e.g., 03-1234-5678 for landline or 090-1234-5678 for mobile). Include area code and use hyphens to separate number groups as commonly done in Japan.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: Write your phone number in the parentheses. Include your area code and write the number in the standard Japanese format (e.g., 03-1234-5678 for landline or 090-1234-5678 for mobile).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.10: Enter the phone number of your workplace or employer. Write the number in standard Japanese format, typically including the area code.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.11: Enter your home phone number or mobile phone number where you can be reached. Use the standard Japanese phone number format with hyphens between number groups.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.3: Enter the date when you obtained your qualification or lost your job using the format year・month・day with dots between numbers.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.5: Write the specific documents you're attaching, such as unemployment certificate, retirement certificate, qualification verification documents, academic certificates, etc. List each document type in the parentheses provided.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Write your phone number in the format shown, with area code in parentheses. For Tokyo numbers, use 03 followed by your 8-digit phone number in parentheses like the example.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This indicates 'Form No. 1' - you don't need to fill this in as it's a pre-printed form identifier.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This is the form title 'Application for National Health Insurance Certificate (Emergency Use)' - no action needed, it's pre-printed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Write '宛先' (addressed to) followed by your ward mayor, typically '板橋区長 様' (To the Mayor of Itabashi Ward).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write '申請日' (Application Date) - this is just a label, the actual date goes in field 5.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Enter today's date in Japanese format: year, month, day using Japanese numerals or Arabic numbers.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Write 'フリガナ' (Furigana) - this is just a label for the phonetic reading of your name that goes above.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Write your birth year, month, and day in the format shown.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Circle the appropriate era: 昭和 (Showa), 平成 (Heisei), 令和 (Reiwa), or 西暦 (Western calendar) that corresponds to your birth year.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Write '世帯主氏名' (Head of Household Name) - enter the name of the household head.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: This label '証明対象世帯' means 'Household Subject to Certification' - the actual household information goes in the rows below.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write your address starting with '板橋区' followed by your specific district, street number, and apartment details.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Write '万書' if applicable, or leave blank - this refers to a specific document number format.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Write '被保険者等記号・番号' (Insured Person Symbol/Number) - enter your health insurance identification numbers in the format '19--'.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: Write your phone number using the dash format shown (e.g., 03-1234-5678). If you don't have a phone number, you can leave it blank or write 'なし' (none).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.10: Fill in the circle (○) that applies to your situation from the options provided in this section. Use a black pen to completely fill in the circle for your selection.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: This indicates what the certificates are needed for - write the purpose such as 'tax filing', 'visa application', 'employment', etc.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.9: This is informational text stating that the processing fee is 300 yen per certificate - no action needed, just be prepared to pay this amount for each document you request.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.4: This field is for the government office clerk or staff member who processes your application to sign their name or stamp. As an applicant, you should leave this field blank - it will be filled out by the official handling your paperwork.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Write the title of the form at the top - this appears to be a form for National Health Insurance (国民健康保険) eligibility loss notification.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Enter your National Health Insurance card number - this is the identification number found on your insurance card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Write today's date when you are filling out this form, in Japanese date format (year/month/day).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Check the box that applies to your area - 江東区 (Koto Ward) is pre-filled, but there are options for 全部 (all) or 一部 (partial).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: This appears to be part of the area selection - leave blank unless instructed otherwise by the office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Enter your insurance card symbol number (記号) - this is typically '08' as pre-filled on many forms.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Write the current year using the Japanese era system (like Reiwa 5) or Western calendar as preferred by your local office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Write 届出人 (applicant/person submitting) - this indicates who is filing the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Write your name as the person submitting this form - use the same name as on your insurance card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Check 世帯主 if you are the head of household, 本人 if you are the insured person yourself, or その他 if neither applies.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write your current address where you live - include postal code, prefecture, city, and apartment details.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: This appears to be a continuation or specification related to your status - follow office guidance for this field.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Write your full name as it appears on official documents - use katakana if you are a foreign resident.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Write additional information in parentheses if 'その他' (other) was selected above - specify your relationship to the insured person.

This is typically pre-printed on the form, so you usually don't need to write anything here yourself.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Write the current date in Japanese format (year.month.day) using Japanese era year (e.g., 令和5.12.15 for December 15, 2023). You can ask at the ward office counter for help with the correct era year format.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: This field is for indicating what documents you are submitting or referencing with this form. You would typically write the names of any supporting documents you're including, such as residence card, passport, or other required paperwork specified in the form instructions.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.9: Write your date of birth in the format MM.DD (month.day) using two digits for each. For example, if born on March 5th, write '03.05'.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.11: Write your date of birth in the format YYYY.MM.DD (year.month.day) using the Japanese calendar system or Western calendar as specified. Use four digits for year and two digits each for month and day.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.14: Write your date of birth in MM.DD format (month.day) using two digits for each field. This appears to be for a dependent or family member's information.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: This appears to be an instruction field indicating 'Fill in Book 3' - you may need to reference a separate instruction booklet or this may indicate which section of a multi-part form you're completing.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: This looks like a field for entering dates or numerical values with specific formatting - fill in the numbers in the designated spaces, likely following a pattern shown in accompanying instructions.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: Similar to field 3, this appears to be for entering numerical data with a specific format - check the form instructions for the exact type of information required (possibly dates, ID numbers, or reference codes).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.4: Write the date you moved to your current address in Japanese format (year.month.day), using the Japanese era year system (e.g., Reiwa 5.4.15 for April 15, 2023).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.11: Enter the date you moved from your previous address in the same Japanese format (year.month.day). This should be the same date as or close to field 4.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.12: Write the complete address of where you lived before moving to your current address. Use Japanese characters and follow the standard Japanese address format from largest to smallest administrative divisions.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.15: Enter the date range when you lived at the previous address, showing the period from when you first moved there until when you left (format: year.month.day to year.month.day).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.1: Write the date you moved into your current address in Japanese date format (year.month.day). Use the Japanese calendar year (Reiwa era) or Western calendar year as specified on the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.5: Write the date you moved out of your previous address in the same format as field 1. This should be the same date or one day before your move-in date.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.8: Write the date you are submitting this notification form to the municipal office. Use the same date format as the previous fields.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.15: Check the box that applies to your situation - whether you are moving into a special ward (転入) or moving out of a special ward (転出). Most foreign residents will check the first box (転入) when registering a new address.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.3: Check this box if you are moving into this municipality from outside Japan (from abroad). This would apply to foreign residents who are registering their address in Japan for the first time or moving from overseas to this specific municipality.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.1: Check this box if you are moving into this municipality from abroad (overseas). This is commonly used by foreign residents registering their address for the first time in Japan.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.13: This section is for official use only - municipal office staff will fill this out when they process and issue your residence registration. Leave this blank.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.2: This appears to be a checkbox or field related to retroactive application status. If you're applying for benefits that should not be applied retroactively to past periods, this would be marked accordingly.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.3: Enter the identification number of the covered individual and mark whether you are filling this out for yourself or as a representative. If filling for yourself, mark the 'self' option; if for family member or as an authorized representative, mark accordingly.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.6: This refers to processing speed preference at the government office window. Choose standard processing unless you have urgent circumstances that require expedited handling.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.3: This appears to be a checkbox or field related to non-retroactive status. If you're a new applicant or this doesn't apply to your situation, you can likely leave this blank or unchecked.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.4: This section seems to relate to identification numbers and representative information. Enter your resident card number or relevant ID, and if you're filling this out yourself (not through a representative), mark the 'self' option.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.7: This appears to be related to processing speed or urgency. Choose standard processing unless you have a specific need for expedited service, which may involve additional fees.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: This section tracks the collection and issuance of documents during the application process. Leave blank initially - office staff will fill this out when they collect your application and issue receipts or certificates.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.12: Check this box if you want to receive documents at the service counter in person. This is often faster and ensures you get your documents directly from staff.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.15: Check this box if you want documents mailed to your registered address. Make sure your address is current in the resident registry before selecting this option.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Check this box if you want to receive notifications and documents by mail/postal delivery. This is typically the most common delivery method selected by residents who want official correspondence sent to their registered address.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: Select whether you want to receive documents by mail (郵送) or pick them up in person. Most foreign residents should choose mail delivery (郵送) for convenience unless they prefer to collect documents directly from the office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.2: This appears to be another delivery method selection field - choose mail delivery (郵送) if you want documents sent to your registered address, which is typically the most practical option for foreign residents.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.6: Check this box if you are submitting the form at a service counter/window in person, as opposed to submitting by mail or other means. This indicates the method of submission rather than requiring you to write anything.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.1: Write the date when your health insurance coverage began in YYYY/MM/DD format. If you're unsure of the exact date, check your insurance card or contact your employer's HR department.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.2: Write the date when your health insurance coverage ended in YYYY/MM/DD format. Leave this blank if your coverage is still active and ongoing.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.1: Write the date when your health insurance coverage began. Use the Japanese date format (year/month/day) and refer to your health insurance card for the exact start date.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.2: Write the date when your health insurance coverage ended or will end. If your coverage is still active, leave this field blank or write the expected termination date if known.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.6: This connects to the requirement above - you must submit a copy of your health insurance qualification loss certificate along with the other required documents listed in this form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.2: Check this box only if you are reporting the death of a household member. This would be used when someone in your household has passed away and you need to update the resident registration accordingly.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Write your household registration number (世帯の記号番号) - this is the unique identifier assigned to your household by the ward office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This is a pre-printed form identifier indicating 'Form No. 3 (Related to Article 30)' - you don't need to fill this in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This form is addressed to the Setagaya Ward Mayor - this is pre-printed and doesn't need to be filled out.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write the date you are submitting this form (year/month/day) in the Japanese format.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Write the effective date of the insurance change (year/month/day) - this is when your national health insurance status will change.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Write your name in katakana (フリガナ) - the phonetic reading of your name in Japanese characters.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Write your full name as it appears on your residence card or other official documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Write 'submitter' or 'applicant' (届出人) - this indicates who is filing the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Write your relationship to the household head (世帯主との続柄) - such as 'head' (世帯主), 'spouse' (配偶者), etc.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Write your phone number including area code in case the office needs to contact you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write your full address including postal code - this should match your registered address.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Check the circle that corresponds to your request: 1=enrollment, 2=withdrawal, 3=status change, 4=other (specify).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: This instruction asks you to circle (○) the content of your submission from the options above.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Note that if the household head has the same address as the applicant, you don't need to write the address again.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Write the district/ward name and apartment/house number of the household head's address.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.10: Circle the appropriate option if you are moving in from or moving out to a special ward, domestic location, or overseas location. This is typically handled automatically when you submit moving notifications at the municipal office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.15: Circle this if there are changes to your household composition such as changing the head of household, merging households, or splitting into separate households. Most foreign residents can leave this blank unless specifically making household structure changes.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.3: Check this box and fill in details if you're changing who is the head of household, merging households, or splitting into separate households. Leave blank if this doesn't apply to your situation.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.8: This section is for restoration of record by official authority or moving within the same city/town. Most foreign residents can leave this blank unless specifically instructed by city hall staff to fill it out.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: This indicates it's an example form for reference - you don't need to fill this part out on your actual form.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: Write your household registration number (世帯番号) which can be found on your residence certificate or previous documents from the ward office.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: This shows the form type and legal reference - no action needed, it's pre-printed information.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: This is the form title 'National Health Insurance Change of Address Form' - pre-printed, no filling required.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.5: Write the date you're submitting this form in Japanese format (year/month/day).
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: Write your name in katakana phonetic spelling (フリガナ) above the name field.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.7: Check this box if you are the head of household (世帯主) submitting the form yourself.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: Write your full name in kanji or your registered name exactly as it appears on your residence card.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.9: Write your current address in Japanese, including the ward, district, and building/apartment number.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.10: This section is for the submitting person's information - fill if different from the person the form is about.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.11: Write your phone number including area code for the office to contact you if needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.12: Write your current address again in the designated address field format.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.13: Circle (1) for '加入' (enrollment/joining) if you're newly registering for national health insurance.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.14: Provide any additional required documents or information as specified in the submission requirements.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.12: This field is pre-printed with '本人' (self/head of household) indicating this row is for the primary person on the form. No action needed - this identifies that row 1 is for the main applicant or household head.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.1: Write the full name of your spouse exactly as it appears on their National Health Insurance card or residence card. If your spouse doesn't have National Health Insurance (for example, if they have employee health insurance through work), you may leave this blank or write 'N/A'.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.1: Write the name of the family member as it appears on their National Health Insurance card. This should match exactly with their registered name in the health insurance system.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.5: This is a pre-printed checkbox or field indicating the type of notification being sent. As a form recipient, you typically don't need to fill this out - it's marked by the government office to indicate this is an official notice or information document.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.7: This appears to be a checkbox or signature area for a transfer agreement form, though the text is partially corrupted with missing characters (indicated by □ symbols). If this is an active field, you would typically check a box to indicate agreement or sign to authorize the transfer of your registration information between municipal offices.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.6: Write your My Number (マイナンバー) - the 12-digit individual identification number assigned to all residents of Japan. If you don't have your My Number card with you, you can find this number on your residence card or notification card.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: This field is for indicating the reason for household registration changes (世帯合併 - household merger). Write the specific reason for combining households, such as marriage, moving in with family, or other circumstances that resulted in merging separate household registrations into one.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: This is Box 2 (枠2) which appears to be a section for personal information including gender (男・女), era/year/month/day date fields, and other basic details. Fill in the requested personal information in the designated subsections within this box, following the Japanese date format using era years (like Reiwa) if required.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.7: This is Box 2 (枠2) which appears to be for entering personal information of individuals being added to the household register, including details like name, gender, birth date, and relationship to the household head.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.12: This field indicates the status of storage/collection handover - mark either '済' (completed) if the process is finished or '不要' (not required) if this step doesn't apply to your situation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.12: This field indicates the insurance copayment rate before any changes. If you previously had a 20% copayment rate for medical expenses under your health insurance, write '2割' (20%). Leave blank if this is your first enrollment or if no previous rate applies.

538501fc448fca9cf5bceb1a9bae69c9.en: (        ) Completed・Not completed (Lost・Envelope already given)・Old certificate (Expired)
538501fc448fca9cf5bceb1a9bae69c9.note: Check the appropriate status of your residence card or certificate - whether processing is completed, if you lost it or already received the envelope, or if you have an expired old certificate.

8853579aa9bc54cb120140a2eef4d43e.note: This appears to be a field where you specify what type of document you want issued or who should be notified - leave blank unless specifically instructed by staff

9e7657719ea1c5c5c3635f6247035588.note: Enter the month for which you're submitting the payment slip, if applicable to your registration change

c1c84d0947f7631fb5035394a708ce29.note: Matched: 有・無・不明, 有・無, 不明

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This indicates the form number and legal reference - you don't need to fill this in as it's pre-printed identification showing this is Form 9 related to Article 3, Paragraph 1 of the relevant regulations.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Write the date when you are submitting this form, typically in Japanese format (year/month/day using the Japanese calendar era).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Write the name of your municipal mayor or the official title of whoever will receive this form at your local city/ward office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This is the main title indicating this form is for reporting the end of National Health Insurance eligibility and loss of qualification (due to change of employment status).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Check the appropriate box - '全部喪失' means complete loss of coverage, '一部喪失' means partial loss of coverage.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Write the date when your health insurance coverage ended or will end, in Japanese date format.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Fill in your ward/district name where you live.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Write your complete address including street number and apartment/building name if applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Enter your resident registration number or insurance member number as it appears on your insurance card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Write the year when your coverage ended.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write the month when your coverage ended.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Write the day when your coverage ended.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Write the year when you are submitting this application.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Write the month when you are submitting this application.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Write the day when you are submitting this application.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.16: This section is for your record number - fill in if you know it from previous correspondence.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.17: Check '有' (yes) if you have a disability certificate, or '無' (no) if you don't have one.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.13: Write the specific reason for the notification or change being reported on this form. This could be moving addresses, change in household composition, or other administrative matters - refer to the form's main purpose or instructions to determine what category applies to your situation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Write the name of the head of household or the person responsible for submitting this resident registration form. This should be written in katakana if you are a foreign resident.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.14: This indicates you are filling out the second box/section of the form. No action needed - this is just a reference marker for the form section.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: This appears to be a field for specifying 'other' category or additional information related to the form section. If none of the pre-printed options apply to your situation, write the appropriate category or details that best describes your circumstances in this blank field.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.3: This appears to be a blank field for additional information or specifications related to the form section. Leave blank unless you have specific additional details that don't fit in the standard categories, or follow any accompanying instructions that specify what should be written here.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.7: This appears to be a field for additional notes or remarks related to the deceased person's information. Leave blank unless you have specific additional information that needs to be recorded about the person who passed away.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.6: Write the telephone number where you can be reached during the day. Include the area code and write numbers clearly using Arabic numerals.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.12: This field asks for information about household head relationship details, but notes that if you already filled this information in the section above, you can skip this field entirely.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.5: Check this box if you plan to move out of your current address in the future. This is used to notify the municipality of your intention to relocate, which helps with administrative planning and may be required for certain procedures.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.1: Check this box if you are planning to move out of your current address to a different municipality. This is used to notify the local government of your intention to relocate so they can process the necessary paperwork for your residence record transfer.

08a1c5434aa3b7f2372ed86cb19d6629.en: National Health Insurance / Date of loss / National Health Insurance
08a1c5434aa3b7f2372ed86cb19d6629.note: Matched: 国民健康保険, 喪失年月日, 国民健康

378d4937dfbe063a21f32c8ba4a5b1ed.note: This indicates information hasn't been processed yet in the system - no action needed on your part, just wait for processing to complete.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This is an administrative section for government information sharing systems. As a regular applicant, you typically don't need to fill anything in this section - it's used by government offices to coordinate data between different agencies.
